questions.prompt: The Italian Renaissance began in city-states such as Florence primarily because
choices: these cities had grown wealthy through trade and banking
the Roman Catholic Church discouraged learning elsewhere
feudal lords sponsored universities in rural areas
the Black Death had spared only northern Italy
questions.correctText: these cities had grown wealthy through trade and banking

questions.prompt: The Medici family is best known for
choices: leading a peasant revolt against the papacy
serving as wealthy patrons of Renaissance artists in Florence
translating the Bible into German
discovering a sea route to India
questions.correctText: serving as wealthy patrons of Renaissance artists in Florence

questions.prompt: Humanism, the dominant intellectual movement of the Renaissance, emphasized
choices: the study of classical Greek and Roman texts and human potential
strict obedience to medieval Church doctrine
the rejection of art and literature as worldly distractions
the divine right of monarchs to rule
questions.correctText: the study of classical Greek and Roman texts and human potential

questions.prompt: Leonardo da Vinci is often considered the embodiment of the Renaissance ideal because he
choices: led the Catholic Reformation in Italy
excelled in art, science, and engineering
discovered the Americas for Spain
wrote a guide for ruthless rulers
questions.correctText: excelled in art, science, and engineering

questions.prompt: Michelangelo's painting of the Sistine Chapel ceiling reflects Renaissance values by
choices: depicting the human form with realism and classical influence
rejecting all religious subject matter
promoting Protestant theology
showing only abstract geometric designs
questions.correctText: depicting the human form with realism and classical influence

questions.prompt: In The Prince, Niccolò Machiavelli argued that a successful ruler should
choices: always act morally regardless of consequences
do whatever is necessary to maintain power and stability
share power equally with the people
defer all decisions to the Church
questions.correctText: do whatever is necessary to maintain power and stability

questions.prompt: The Northern Renaissance differed from the Italian Renaissance in that it
choices: placed greater emphasis on religious reform and everyday life
rejected the use of oil paints
had no contact with classical learning
ignored printing technology
questions.correctText: placed greater emphasis on religious reform and everyday life

questions.prompt: Desiderius Erasmus, a leading Northern Renaissance thinker, is best known for
choices: writing In Praise of Folly and criticizing Church abuses
leading the Spanish Inquisition
designing the dome of the Florence Cathedral
circumnavigating the globe
questions.correctText: writing In Praise of Folly and criticizing Church abuses

questions.prompt: Sir Thomas More's Utopia is significant because it
choices: imagined an ideal society and criticized the problems of his own
served as a manual for absolute monarchs
denounced classical Greek philosophy
called for the conquest of the Americas
questions.correctText: imagined an ideal society and criticized the problems of his own

questions.prompt: Johannes Gutenberg's printing press, developed around 1450, had its greatest impact by
choices: making books and ideas widely and cheaply available
limiting literacy to the clergy
ending the Renaissance in Italy
preventing the spread of the Protestant Reformation
questions.correctText: making books and ideas widely and cheaply available

questions.prompt: One major reason the printing press contributed to the Protestant Reformation was that it
choices: allowed Martin Luther's writings to spread rapidly across Europe
translated all Catholic services into Latin
was monopolized by the papacy
destroyed earlier manuscript traditions overnight
questions.correctText: allowed Martin Luther's writings to spread rapidly across Europe

questions.prompt: Martin Luther posted his Ninety-Five Theses in 1517 mainly to protest
choices: the sale of indulgences by the Catholic Church
the rule of the Holy Roman Emperor
the discovery of the New World
the work of Galileo Galilei
questions.correctText: the sale of indulgences by the Catholic Church

questions.prompt: A central teaching of Martin Luther was that salvation comes through
choices: faith in God alone, not through good works or indulgences
obedience to the pope above all rulers
purchasing pardons from the Church
membership in monastic orders
questions.correctText: faith in God alone, not through good works or indulgences

questions.prompt: John Calvin's doctrine of predestination taught that
choices: God had already chosen who would be saved
all people could earn salvation through good works
the pope could grant salvation for a fee
salvation depended on participation in the sacraments alone
questions.correctText: God had already chosen who would be saved

questions.prompt: Henry VIII of England broke with the Roman Catholic Church primarily because
choices: the pope refused to annul his marriage to Catherine of Aragon
he wished to translate the Bible into Latin
he supported Martin Luther's theology
Spain demanded that England leave the Church
questions.correctText: the pope refused to annul his marriage to Catherine of Aragon

questions.prompt: The Act of Supremacy (1534) established that
choices: the English monarch was the head of the Church of England
Parliament could overrule the king on religion
Catholicism was outlawed throughout Europe
the pope controlled English bishops
questions.correctText: the English monarch was the head of the Church of England

questions.prompt: The Counter-Reformation refers to the Catholic Church's effort to
choices: respond to Protestantism through reform and missionary work
merge permanently with Lutheran churches
abolish the office of pope
support the spread of Calvinism
questions.correctText: respond to Protestantism through reform and missionary work

questions.prompt: The Council of Trent (1545–1563) is best described as a
choices: Catholic council that reaffirmed core doctrines and reformed abuses
treaty ending the Hundred Years' War
Protestant assembly that wrote a new Bible
meeting that created the Society of Jesus
questions.correctText: Catholic council that reaffirmed core doctrines and reformed abuses

questions.prompt: The Society of Jesus (Jesuits), founded by Ignatius of Loyola, focused on
choices: education and Catholic missionary work around the world
leading peasant uprisings in Germany
supporting Henry VIII's break from Rome
translating the Bible into vernacular languages
questions.correctText: education and Catholic missionary work around the world

questions.prompt: The Peace of Augsburg (1555) is significant because it
choices: allowed German princes to choose Lutheranism or Catholicism for their states
ended the Thirty Years' War
united all of Christendom under the pope
outlawed Protestantism in Europe
questions.correctText: allowed German princes to choose Lutheranism or Catholicism for their states

questions.prompt: A long-term political result of the Protestant Reformation was
choices: the weakening of papal authority and the rise of national churches
the unification of all European Christians
the abolition of monarchies across Europe
the end of religious warfare on the continent
questions.correctText: the weakening of papal authority and the rise of national churches

questions.prompt: Nicolaus Copernicus challenged traditional astronomy by proposing that
choices: the Sun, not Earth, was at the center of the solar system
Earth was a flat disk
the planets moved in perfect squares
comets caused all earthly events
questions.correctText: the Sun, not Earth, was at the center of the solar system

questions.prompt: Galileo Galilei's use of the telescope provided evidence supporting
choices: the heliocentric model of the universe
the geocentric model defended by the Church
the existence of only seven planets
the rotation of the Sun around Earth
questions.correctText: the heliocentric model of the universe

questions.prompt: Galileo's conflict with the Catholic Church in 1633 illustrates
choices: the tension between traditional authority and new scientific ideas
the Church's full embrace of Copernican astronomy
the success of the Counter-Reformation in Italy
the unimportance of telescopic observation
questions.correctText: the tension between traditional authority and new scientific ideas

questions.prompt: Sir Isaac Newton's Principia Mathematica is most associated with
choices: the laws of motion and universal gravitation
the heliocentric theory of Copernicus
the invention of the printing press
the doctrine of predestination
questions.correctText: the laws of motion and universal gravitation

questions.prompt: The scientific method developed during the Scientific Revolution emphasized
choices: observation, experimentation, and reasoning based on evidence
reliance on ancient authorities such as Aristotle
the use of prayer to explain natural events
the rejection of mathematics in science
questions.correctText: observation, experimentation, and reasoning based on evidence

questions.prompt: A major long-term effect of the Scientific Revolution was
choices: a growing belief that reason could explain the natural world
the strengthening of feudal institutions
a return to medieval scholasticism
the rejection of all classical learning
questions.correctText: a growing belief that reason could explain the natural world

questions.prompt: European motives for overseas exploration in the late 1400s are often summarized as
choices: God, gold, and glory
land, liberty, and law
freedom, fraternity, and equality
honor, courage, and tradition
questions.correctText: God, gold, and glory

questions.prompt: Prince Henry the Navigator of Portugal is best known for
choices: sponsoring a school of navigation and Atlantic voyages
discovering the Pacific Ocean
circumnavigating the globe
conquering the Aztec Empire
questions.correctText: sponsoring a school of navigation and Atlantic voyages

questions.prompt: Vasco da Gama's voyage in 1498 was significant because it
choices: opened a direct sea route from Europe to India around Africa
led to the conquest of the Inca Empire
discovered Brazil for Portugal
established the first European colony in North America
questions.correctText: opened a direct sea route from Europe to India around Africa

questions.prompt: Christopher Columbus's 1492 voyage, sponsored by Spain, was intended to
choices: find a westward sea route to Asia
claim land for Portugal in Africa
circumnavigate the globe in a single trip
destroy the Ottoman Empire
questions.correctText: find a westward sea route to Asia

questions.prompt: The caravel and the astrolabe contributed to European exploration by
choices: improving the ability to sail long distances and navigate at sea
ending European dependence on horses
allowing only short coastal voyages
replacing the compass entirely
questions.correctText: improving the ability to sail long distances and navigate at sea

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Tordesillas (1494) is best described as an agreement that
choices: divided newly discovered lands between Spain and Portugal
ended the Hundred Years' War
established the Holy Roman Empire
united England and Scotland
questions.correctText: divided newly discovered lands between Spain and Portugal

questions.prompt: Ferdinand Magellan's expedition (1519–1522) is historically significant because it
choices: completed the first circumnavigation of the globe
discovered the Mississippi River
conquered the Aztec capital
ended Portuguese influence in Africa
questions.correctText: completed the first circumnavigation of the globe

questions.prompt: Hernán Cortés is most directly associated with
choices: the Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire
the founding of Jamestown
the circumnavigation of the globe
the conquest of the Inca Empire
questions.correctText: the Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire

questions.prompt: Francisco Pizarro is best known for
choices: leading the Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire
founding the colony of New France
negotiating the Treaty of Tordesillas
leading the first voyage around Africa
questions.correctText: leading the Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire

questions.prompt: The Columbian Exchange refers to the
choices: transfer of plants, animals, people, and diseases between the Eastern and Western Hemispheres
trade in textiles between India and England
exchange of ideas between Christian and Muslim scholars
diplomatic exchange between Spain and Portugal
questions.correctText: transfer of plants, animals, people, and diseases between the Eastern and Western Hemispheres

questions.prompt: One devastating result of European contact for indigenous peoples of the Americas was
choices: catastrophic population loss due to diseases such as smallpox
the immediate adoption of European political systems
the development of industrial cities
a sharp rise in indigenous birth rates
questions.correctText: catastrophic population loss due to diseases such as smallpox

questions.prompt: The encomienda system in Spanish America required
choices: indigenous people to provide labor and tribute to Spanish landholders
Spanish settlers to pay taxes to native rulers
free movement of African slaves to Europe
the equal sharing of land between settlers and natives
questions.correctText: indigenous people to provide labor and tribute to Spanish landholders

questions.prompt: Bartolomé de las Casas is best remembered for
choices: criticizing the mistreatment of indigenous peoples under Spanish rule
leading the conquest of the Aztecs
founding the Society of Jesus
writing The Prince
questions.correctText: criticizing the mistreatment of indigenous peoples under Spanish rule

questions.prompt: The Atlantic slave trade expanded most rapidly because European colonies in the Americas needed
choices: labor for plantations producing sugar, tobacco, and cotton
soldiers to fight in European wars
missionaries to convert local populations
skilled artisans to build cathedrals
questions.correctText: labor for plantations producing sugar, tobacco, and cotton

questions.prompt: The Middle Passage is best described as
choices: the brutal voyage of enslaved Africans across the Atlantic to the Americas
the trade route between Spain and the Philippines
the path Spanish silver took to China
the European invasion route into the Ottoman Empire
questions.correctText: the brutal voyage of enslaved Africans across the Atlantic to the Americas

questions.prompt: Mercantilism, the dominant economic theory of the 1600s and 1700s, held that a country's wealth was measured by its
choices: supply of gold and silver and its favorable balance of trade
size of its peasant population
number of religious orders
investment in foreign manufacturing
questions.correctText: supply of gold and silver and its favorable balance of trade

questions.prompt: Under mercantilist policy, colonies existed primarily to
choices: supply raw materials to and buy finished goods from the mother country
develop independent industries to rival Europe
trade freely with any nation they chose
spread democratic government
questions.correctText: supply raw materials to and buy finished goods from the mother country

questions.prompt: The triangular trade linked
choices: Europe, Africa, and the Americas through goods, slaves, and raw materials
Russia, China, and Japan in a silk trade
the Mediterranean and the Indian Ocean in a spice trade
Spain, France, and Britain in a wool trade only
questions.correctText: Europe, Africa, and the Americas through goods, slaves, and raw materials

questions.prompt: The Mughal Empire under Akbar the Great is remembered for its
choices: policy of religious toleration and effective administration
destruction of all Hindu temples
rejection of Persian culture
withdrawal from foreign trade
questions.correctText: policy of religious toleration and effective administration

questions.prompt: The Taj Mahal, built under Mughal emperor Shah Jahan, is best understood as
choices: a mausoleum honoring his wife and a masterpiece of Islamic architecture
the capital city of the Mughal Empire
a fortress built to defend Delhi from invaders
a Hindu temple converted into a mosque
questions.correctText: a mausoleum honoring his wife and a masterpiece of Islamic architecture

questions.prompt: The reign of Mughal emperor Aurangzeb is often associated with
choices: religious intolerance and growing strains on the empire
the founding of the Mughal dynasty
the construction of the Taj Mahal
an alliance with the Ottoman Empire
questions.correctText: religious intolerance and growing strains on the empire

questions.prompt: Suleiman the Magnificent is best known for
choices: leading the Ottoman Empire to its political and cultural peak
conquering the Inca Empire
leading the Protestant Reformation
founding the Mughal Empire
questions.correctText: leading the Ottoman Empire to its political and cultural peak

questions.prompt: Mehmed II is most famous for
choices: conquering Constantinople in 1453 and ending the Byzantine Empire
circumnavigating the globe
writing the Quran
founding the Safavid Empire
questions.correctText: conquering Constantinople in 1453 and ending the Byzantine Empire

questions.prompt: The Ottoman conquest of Constantinople in 1453 had which immediate effect on Europe?
choices: It encouraged Europeans to seek new sea routes to Asia
It ended the Renaissance in Italy
It united Christian and Muslim states permanently
It caused the Protestant Reformation
questions.correctText: It encouraged Europeans to seek new sea routes to Asia

questions.prompt: Under the Ming dynasty, the voyages of Zheng He demonstrated that China
choices: possessed advanced ships and could project power across the Indian Ocean
had no interest in foreign lands
depended on European naval technology
had been conquered by the Mongols
questions.correctText: possessed advanced ships and could project power across the Indian Ocean

questions.prompt: The Forbidden City in Beijing served as the
choices: imperial palace complex of the Ming and Qing dynasties
headquarters of the Jesuit missions in China
capital of the Mongol Empire
main port of European traders
questions.correctText: imperial palace complex of the Ming and Qing dynasties

questions.prompt: After Zheng He's voyages ended, Ming China generally followed a policy of
choices: isolation and limited foreign contact
aggressive overseas colonization
open free trade with all nations
alliance with European powers
questions.correctText: isolation and limited foreign contact

questions.prompt: The Qing dynasty, which followed the Ming, was established by
choices: the Manchus from northeast of the Great Wall
Portuguese traders along the coast
Mongol invaders from Central Asia
a peasant revolt led by Buddhist monks
questions.correctText: the Manchus from northeast of the Great Wall

questions.prompt: Tokugawa Japan's policy of sakoku, or 'closed country,' was designed to
choices: limit foreign influence and maintain internal stability
expand Japan's trade with all of Asia
encourage Christian missionaries
promote European-style government
questions.correctText: limit foreign influence and maintain internal stability

questions.prompt: Under sakoku, the main European group allowed limited contact with Japan was the
choices: Dutch, through the port of Nagasaki
Portuguese, through Kyoto
British, through Edo
Spanish, through Hokkaido
questions.correctText: Dutch, through the port of Nagasaki

questions.prompt: Absolutism is best defined as a form of government in which
choices: a monarch holds total power, usually claiming divine right
elected officials share power with the king
the church alone rules the state
noble assemblies dominate decision-making
questions.correctText: a monarch holds total power, usually claiming divine right

questions.prompt: The phrase 'L'état, c'est moi' ('I am the state') is associated with
choices: Louis XIV of France
Peter the Great of Russia
Henry VIII of England
Charles I of England
questions.correctText: Louis XIV of France

questions.prompt: Louis XIV's Palace of Versailles served mainly to
choices: showcase royal power and keep the nobility under royal control
house France's first parliament
serve as a Catholic monastery
act as a fortress against Spanish invasion
questions.correctText: showcase royal power and keep the nobility under royal control

questions.prompt: Peter the Great of Russia is best known for
choices: modernizing and westernizing Russia in the late 1600s and early 1700s
leading the French Revolution
founding the Ottoman Empire
writing The Wealth of Nations
questions.correctText: modernizing and westernizing Russia in the late 1600s and early 1700s

questions.prompt: Peter the Great founded the city of St. Petersburg as a
choices: 'window on the West' to connect Russia with Europe
religious capital for the Orthodox Church
fortress against Mongol invaders
trading post for Chinese goods
questions.correctText: 'window on the West' to connect Russia with Europe

questions.prompt: The Habsburg family rose to power in Europe primarily through
choices: a series of strategic marriages and inherited territories
election by the people of Spain
leading the Protestant Reformation
founding the Society of Jesus
questions.correctText: a series of strategic marriages and inherited territories

questions.prompt: Absolute monarchs justified their authority through the doctrine of divine right, which held that
choices: a king's power came directly from God
kings were elected by nobles
the pope appointed all rulers
parliament limited royal authority
questions.correctText: a king's power came directly from God

questions.prompt: The English Civil War (1642–1651) was fought largely over
choices: the limits of royal authority and the rights of Parliament
control of overseas colonies in Africa
religious differences between Catholics and Jews
the unification of England and France
questions.correctText: the limits of royal authority and the rights of Parliament

questions.prompt: The execution of King Charles I in 1649 is significant because it
choices: showed that even a monarch could be held accountable to law
ended the English monarchy permanently
began the Hundred Years' War
established absolute monarchy in England
questions.correctText: showed that even a monarch could be held accountable to law

questions.prompt: The Glorious Revolution of 1688 resulted in
choices: the removal of James II and the strengthening of Parliament
the restoration of absolute monarchy in England
England leaving the Protestant faith
Scotland's independence from England
questions.correctText: the removal of James II and the strengthening of Parliament

questions.prompt: The English Bill of Rights (1689) most directly limited the monarch by
choices: requiring the consent of Parliament for taxes and standing armies
abolishing the Church of England
ending all trade with the colonies
creating a written constitution like that of the United States
questions.correctText: requiring the consent of Parliament for taxes and standing armies

questions.prompt: The Enlightenment is best described as an intellectual movement that emphasized
choices: reason, natural rights, and the use of science to improve society
obedience to traditional religious authority
the divine right of monarchs
a return to medieval feudalism
questions.correctText: reason, natural rights, and the use of science to improve society

questions.prompt: John Locke argued that government exists to
choices: protect the natural rights of life, liberty, and property
enforce the will of the established church
serve the absolute will of a divinely chosen king
redistribute wealth among the peasantry
questions.correctText: protect the natural rights of life, liberty, and property

questions.prompt: Baron de Montesquieu's most influential idea was the
choices: separation of governmental powers into legislative, executive, and judicial branches
abolition of all monarchies
concept of laissez-faire economics
social contract between ruler and ruled
questions.correctText: separation of governmental powers into legislative, executive, and judicial branches

questions.prompt: Voltaire is best known for his defense of
choices: freedom of speech and religious toleration
the absolute power of monarchs
Catholic missionary activity
the encomienda system
questions.correctText: freedom of speech and religious toleration

questions.prompt: Jean-Jacques Rousseau's idea of the 'social contract' held that
choices: government derives its authority from the consent of the governed
monarchs rule by direct command of God
trade should be regulated by mercantilist policies
rulers should follow the teachings of Machiavelli
questions.correctText: government derives its authority from the consent of the governed

questions.prompt: In The Wealth of Nations, Adam Smith argued that economies grow best when
choices: governments allow free markets to operate with limited interference
the state controls all production and trade
guilds set fixed prices and wages
colonies are forced to buy only from the mother country
questions.correctText: governments allow free markets to operate with limited interference

questions.prompt: The American Revolution is often described as a product of Enlightenment ideas because
choices: the Declaration of Independence draws on natural rights and consent of the governed
it rejected representative government
it restored monarchy to the colonies
it relied on mercantilist theory
questions.correctText: the Declaration of Independence draws on natural rights and consent of the governed

questions.prompt: The French Revolution officially began in 1789 with
choices: the meeting of the Estates-General and the storming of the Bastille
the execution of Louis XVI
Napoleon's coronation as emperor
the Congress of Vienna
questions.correctText: the meeting of the Estates-General and the storming of the Bastille

questions.prompt: The French Third Estate represented
choices: commoners, including peasants, urban workers, and the middle class
the Catholic clergy
the nobility only
foreign ambassadors at Versailles
questions.correctText: commoners, including peasants, urban workers, and the middle class

questions.prompt: The storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789, has become a symbol of
choices: the people's revolt against royal tyranny
the restoration of the monarchy
the founding of the Catholic Church in France
the beginning of the Napoleonic Wars
questions.correctText: the people's revolt against royal tyranny

questions.prompt: The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen (1789) reflected Enlightenment ideas by
choices: proclaiming liberty, equality, and natural rights for citizens
restoring the privileges of the nobility
declaring the king's absolute authority
establishing the Catholic Church as the state religion
questions.correctText: proclaiming liberty, equality, and natural rights for citizens

questions.prompt: The Reign of Terror during the French Revolution was characterized by
choices: mass executions ordered by the Committee of Public Safety
the restoration of Louis XVI to power
peaceful negotiations with Austria and Prussia
the spread of Lutheran reform
questions.correctText: mass executions ordered by the Committee of Public Safety

questions.prompt: Napoleon Bonaparte's most significant domestic accomplishment was
choices: the creation of the Napoleonic Code, a uniform system of laws
writing the Declaration of Independence
establishing parliamentary democracy in France
ending slavery in all French colonies permanently
questions.correctText: the creation of the Napoleonic Code, a uniform system of laws

questions.prompt: The Congress of Vienna (1814–1815) sought to
choices: restore a balance of power and stable monarchies in Europe after Napoleon
spread the French Revolution to all of Europe
create a single European republic
end European involvement in the Americas
questions.correctText: restore a balance of power and stable monarchies in Europe after Napoleon

questions.prompt: Toussaint L'Ouverture is best known for leading
choices: the Haitian Revolution against French colonial rule and slavery
the conquest of Mexico for Spain
the American Revolution against Britain
the unification of Italy
questions.correctText: the Haitian Revolution against French colonial rule and slavery

questions.prompt: Haiti's independence in 1804 is historically significant because it
choices: created the first independent nation born from a successful slave revolt
restored European control over the Caribbean
ended the Atlantic slave trade immediately
joined the United States as a new state
questions.correctText: created the first independent nation born from a successful slave revolt

questions.prompt: Simón Bolívar is often called the 'Liberator' because he
choices: led independence movements in much of northern South America
conquered the Aztec Empire for Spain
served as the first president of Mexico
wrote the Declaration of the Rights of Man
questions.correctText: led independence movements in much of northern South America

questions.prompt: José de San Martín contributed to Latin American independence by
choices: leading liberation campaigns in Argentina, Chile, and Peru
negotiating the Treaty of Tordesillas
writing the Napoleonic Code
founding the Society of Jesus
questions.correctText: leading liberation campaigns in Argentina, Chile, and Peru

questions.prompt: The Industrial Revolution began first in Great Britain mainly because Britain had
choices: natural resources, capital, and stable government to support new industries
the largest colonial empire in Africa by 1750
the only universities in Europe
a peasant economy untouched by trade
questions.correctText: natural resources, capital, and stable government to support new industries

questions.prompt: The enclosure movement in 18th-century Britain
choices: consolidated farmland and pushed many peasants to seek factory work in cities
freed enslaved people throughout the British Empire
expanded common village land for peasants
ended British involvement in trade
questions.correctText: consolidated farmland and pushed many peasants to seek factory work in cities

questions.prompt: James Watt's improvements to the steam engine were significant because they
choices: powered factories, mines, and eventually railways and steamships
ended dependence on coal
made wind power obsolete in agriculture only
replaced human workers entirely in textiles
questions.correctText: powered factories, mines, and eventually railways and steamships

questions.prompt: New textile machinery such as the spinning jenny and power loom transformed industry by
choices: moving cloth production from homes into mechanized factories
ending the demand for cotton
increasing reliance on cottage industries
eliminating the need for raw materials
questions.correctText: moving cloth production from homes into mechanized factories

questions.prompt: A major social effect of the Industrial Revolution was
choices: rapid urbanization as people moved to crowded factory cities
a return to feudal village life
the disappearance of the middle class
an end to long working hours
questions.correctText: rapid urbanization as people moved to crowded factory cities

questions.prompt: Capitalism, as it developed during the Industrial Revolution, is best defined as an economic system based on
choices: private ownership of the means of production and free markets
common ownership of all property
government ownership of all industry
feudal obligations between lords and peasants
questions.correctText: private ownership of the means of production and free markets

questions.prompt: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels, in The Communist Manifesto, argued that history is driven by
choices: class struggle between workers and owners of capital
the divine plan of God
the genius of great kings and emperors
the rise and fall of religious institutions
questions.correctText: class struggle between workers and owners of capital

questions.prompt: Reforms regulating child labor in 19th-century Britain were significant because they
choices: limited working hours and ages for children in factories and mines
encouraged children to work longer shifts
abolished public education
ended the Industrial Revolution
questions.correctText: limited working hours and ages for children in factories and mines

questions.prompt: Labor unions formed during the Industrial Revolution mainly to
choices: secure higher wages, shorter hours, and safer working conditions
support absolute monarchy
abolish private property entirely
promote mercantilism over capitalism
questions.correctText: secure higher wages, shorter hours, and safer working conditions

questions.prompt: The Seneca Falls Convention of 1848 is best known for
choices: launching the organized women's rights movement in the United States
ending child labor in Britain
founding the Society of Jesus
leading to the unification of Germany
questions.correctText: launching the organized women's rights movement in the United States
